page.primary_keyword: World Cup final yacht charter Marbella
page.title: World Cup Final Yacht Charter in Marbella: Watch the Match at Sea

data.meta_description: Book a World Cup final yacht charter in Marbella and watch the match at sea. From €749/2h, with skipper, drinks & snacks included. Depart Puerto Banús.
data.summary: Watch the World Cup final from the water with a private yacht charter in Marbella. Our fleet departs Puerto Banús, with full catering and licensed skipper included.
key_facts: Charter from €749 for 2 hours (Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39) up to €4,719 for 4 hours on a Mangusta 80.
All charters include licensed skipper, fuel, drinks (beer, wine, cava, soft drinks), snacks, insurance, and VAT.
Fleet departs exclusively from Puerto Banús, Marbella – 10 minutes from the Golden Mile.
Choose from 27 boats: from 6.4m speedboats to 29m superyachts, seating 5 to 12 guests.
Free jet ski included on Mangusta 80 charters (minimum 4 hours).
Watch the match on board using your own device or the yacht's TV system (where available).
Book via WhatsApp for instant availability and custom requests.

faq.q: Can I watch the World Cup final live on a yacht charter in Marbella?
faq.a: Yes. You can stream the match on your phone or tablet using the yacht's Wi-Fi (where available) or bring your own device. Some larger yachts like the Ferretti 94 have onboard TV systems – check availability when booking.

faq.q: How much does a World Cup final yacht charter cost in Marbella?
faq.a: Prices start at €749 for 2 hours on the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39, up to €4,719 for 4 hours on a Mangusta 80 (which includes a free jet ski). Other boats are priced on request via WhatsApp.

faq.q: What is included in the charter price?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish VAT (21%). No hidden fees.

faq.q: Which boats are best for a group watching the World Cup final?
faq.a: For groups up to 12, the Mangusta 80 (24m), Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m), or Ferretti 94 (29m) offer ample deck space and comfort. For smaller groups, the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 are great value.

faq.q: Do you provide catering for the match?
faq.a: Light snacks and drinks are included. For a full meal or special menu (e.g., tapas, barbecue), please request via WhatsApp when booking – we can arrange catering at an additional cost.

faq.q: Where does the yacht depart from?
faq.a: All charters depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella. We recommend arriving 15 minutes before departure. Our team will send exact berth details after booking.

faq.q: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.a: Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and non-alcoholic drinks. However, our included bar has plenty of options. Glass bottles are discouraged for safety.

faq.q: How do I book a World Cup final yacht charter?
faq.a: Contact us via WhatsApp for availability, pricing, and to secure your preferred boat. We recommend booking at least 2 weeks in advance for the World Cup final.

data.body_html:
Watching the World Cup final from a private yacht in Marbella combines two of life's greatest pleasures: football and the Mediterranean. With our fleet departing from Puerto Banús, you can anchor off the coast, tune in live, and celebrate with drinks, snacks, and the sea breeze. Whether you're a small group or a dozen friends, we have a boat to match your budget and style.

Why Charter a Yacht for the World Cup Final?

The World Cup final is a global event – why watch it in a crowded bar when you can have your own floating VIP lounge? On a private yacht, you control the atmosphere: no queues, no noisy TVs competing for attention, and uninterrupted views of the Costa del Sol. Anchor in a quiet cove like Cala del Faro or stay close to Puerto Banús for easy access to post-match celebrations. Our licensed skipper handles navigation, so you focus on the game.

Our Fleet for the Final

We offer 27 boats, from 6.4m speedboats to 29m superyachts. Here are the most popular choices for match-day charters:

  • Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) – €749/2h, €2,299/8h. Perfect for small groups on a budget.
  • Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) – same pricing as Astondoa 40. Comfortable flybridge.
  • Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) – €4,719 for 4 hours, includes a free jet ski. Ideal for a premium experience.
  • Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) – spacious flybridge for socialising.
  • Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax) – top-tier luxury with ample deck space.

All charters include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and VAT. For other boats like the Marbella yacht charter fleet, pricing is on request via WhatsApp.

How to Watch the Match Onboard

Most modern yachts have TV screens or media systems. However, to guarantee coverage, we recommend bringing a tablet or smartphone with a mobile data plan. The Costa del Sol has good 4G/5G coverage offshore within a few nautical miles. For larger groups, a portable projector (if the yacht has a white surface) can turn the deck into a cinema.

Sample Itinerary for Match Day

Here's a typical schedule for a 4-hour charter departing Puerto Banús:

  • 14:30 – Board at Puerto Banús. Meet your skipper, stow bags, safety briefing.
  • 15:00 – Depart. Head towards Cabopino or Cala Cortés for a scenic cruise.
  • 16:00 – Anchor for swimming or sunbathing. Drinks and snacks served.
  • 17:30 – Prepare for kick-off. Set up devices, tune in.
  • 18:00 – Watch the match live. Celebrate goals with cava.
  • 19:30 – Cruise back to Puerto Banús, enjoying the sunset over La Concha.
  • 20:00 – Disembark. Option to continue at a beach club.

For longer charters (8 hours), you can add a stop at Estepona or Sotogrande.

What's Included in Every Charter

ItemDetails
Licensed skipperExperienced, English-speaking
FuelFull tank, enough for your itinerary
DrinksWater, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava
SnacksLight bites (olives, crisps, nuts)
InsuranceFull liability and passenger cover
VAT21% Spanish IVA included

No hidden costs. You only pay extra if you want premium catering or a specific bottle of champagne.

Tips for a Great World Cup Final Yacht Experience

  • Book early – the final is the most in-demand day of the year. We recommend at least 2 weeks ahead.
  • Check the weather – April/May finals are usually sunny (20-25°C), but bring a light jacket for evening cruises.
  • Bring a power bank – keep your devices charged for the match.
  • Consider a Mangusta 80 if you want a jet ski for half-time entertainment.
  • Arrive 15 minutes early at Puerto Banús – parking can be busy.

Book Your World Cup Final Yacht Charter

Ready to secure your spot? Contact us via WhatsApp for availability, pricing, and to reserve your preferred boat. We'll confirm your booking with berth details and a pre-charter checklist. For more ideas, read our guide to Marbella yacht charter World Cup final or explore the full Marbella yacht charter fleet.

Frequently Asked Questions

Can I watch the World Cup final live on a yacht charter in Marbella?

Yes. You can stream the match on your phone or tablet using the yacht's Wi-Fi (where available) or bring your own device. Some larger yachts like the Ferretti 94 have onboard TV systems – check availability when booking.

How much does a World Cup final yacht charter cost in Marbella?

Prices start at €749 for 2 hours on the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39, up to €4,719 for 4 hours on a Mangusta 80 (which includes a free jet ski). Other boats are priced on request via WhatsApp.

What is included in the charter price?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish VAT (21%). No hidden fees.

Which boats are best for a group watching the World Cup final?

For groups up to 12, the Mangusta 80 (24m), Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m), or Ferretti 94 (29m) offer ample deck space and comfort. For smaller groups, the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 are great value.

Do you provide catering for the match?

Light snacks and drinks are included. For a full meal or special menu (e.g., tapas, barbecue), please request via WhatsApp when booking – we can arrange catering at an additional cost.

Where does the yacht depart from?

All charters depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella. We recommend arriving 15 minutes before departure. Our team will send exact berth details after booking.

Can I bring my own food and drinks?

Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and non-alcoholic drinks. However, our included bar has plenty of options. Glass bottles are discouraged for safety.

How do I book a World Cup final yacht charter?

Contact us via WhatsApp for availability, pricing, and to secure your preferred boat. We recommend booking at least 2 weeks in advance for the World Cup final.
